pages.name: 01-dashboard
good: Calorie ring with remaining/goal text creates a clear focal point
Green brand color used purposefully for positive metrics (remaining calories, streak)
Water intake section has good progressive disclosure with quick-add buttons
Weight progress chart with goal dashed line adds meaningful context
bad: BMI value (19.8) and 0-day streak are crammed into a small card with poor visual balance — streak '0' feels like an error state not a feature
Macro progress bars are nearly invisible at zero state — thin gray lines with no empty-bar contrast make the section feel broken
Next milestone card uses a dark green bg that is too close in value to the card bg, reducing legibility of '18 weeks away' tag
Dashboard cards have inconsistent internal padding — water intake section feels denser than the calorie ring card
Typography scale is flat — calorie '0' in the ring, macro labels, and section headers all feel similar in weight with no clear hierarchy jump
improvements: Increase calorie ring number to text-5xl font-black and use white for consumed, gray-400 for 'kcal' label to create a clear focal hierarchy
Add a visible track to macro bars (bg-gray-700 h-2 rounded-full) so zero state still shows the full goal bar as a container
Give the streak metric a dedicated icon treatment with a larger number (text-3xl) and add a motivational sub-label like 'Start today!'
Standardize card padding to p-5 across all dashboard cards for visual rhythm
Increase contrast on the milestone card badge '18 weeks away' to bg-green-900 text-green-300 for legibility

pages.name: 02-tracker-today
good: Date navigator with TODAY badge is clean and immediately scannable
Nutrition totals grid (Calories, Protein, Carbs, Fat, Fiber) is well-structured with goal denominators
Search bar placeholder text with Indian food examples (Dal Tadka, Roti, Rice) shows strong localization awareness
Dark card for totals creates good visual separation from the empty list area
bad: Empty state below totals card is just plain text with no illustration or CTA button — 'No food logged today' feels abandoned
The totals card shows all zeros with no visual differentiation between values and goal denominators — they blend together
No meal grouping UI visible (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks) — flat food log architecture reduces usability for Indian multi-meal eating patterns
Settings gear icon top-right has no visible label or tooltip context — unclear what settings are available
Page has too much empty vertical space below the totals card which makes it feel unfinished on longer screens
improvements: Add an illustrated empty state with a bowl/plate icon and a prominent '+ Log your first meal' green CTA button inside the empty area
In the totals grid, style the consumed value as text-white text-xl font-bold and the goal as text-gray-500 text-sm to create clear reading hierarchy
Add collapsible meal sections (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Snacks) with individual calorie subtotals even when empty
Add a recent foods or 'Quick Add' section below search to reduce friction for repeat Indian meals
Make denominator values text-gray-500 text-xs and consumed values text-white text-2xl font-semibold for stronger contrast

pages.name: 05-profile
good: Stats badges row (BMI, TDEE, Target, Protein, Carbs, Fat) provides a compact data-rich summary
BMI healthy weight indicator in green is a great positive reinforcement pattern
Form fields for goal update are logically grouped with clear labels
Dev Mode Active warning banner is visually distinct (orange border) without breaking the layout
bad: Identity card shows only initial avatar 'J' with no option to add a photo — feels placeholder-level for a fitness app
Stats badges have inconsistent unit formatting — '2393 kcal' and '2454 kcal' look nearly identical in size, making TDEE vs Target distinction confusing
The profile header (name, age, height) uses a single muted gray color for all metadata — no visual hierarchy between name and stats
Sign Out button is red text but has no border or background — it looks like a disabled link rather than a destructive action
Re-do Onboarding option is visually identical to Sign Out in weight, making its importance unclear
Form dropdowns (36 weeks, Moderate, Non-Vegetarian) have no visible focus states and low-contrast chevrons
improvements: Differentiate TDEE vs Target badges by labeling TDEE as 'Maintenance' and Target as 'Your Goal' with a green accent border on the Target badge
Make the name 'Jayaraj' text-white text-2xl font-bold and metadata text-gray-400 text-sm to establish hierarchy in the identity card
Give Sign Out a border border-red-800 text-red-400 rounded-lg px-4 py-2 treatment to signal destructive action without aggression
Add avatar upload affordance with a camera icon overlay on the initial circle
Style form selects with a visible bg-gray-800 border border-gray-700 focus:border-green-500 ring for consistent dark-mode form treatment

pages.name: 08-dishes-empty
good: Dish card design is clean with good information density — kcal/100g, macro color coding (P/C/F) works well
Veg badge with green color coding aligns with Indian dietary context perfectly
Edit and delete action icons on the card are positioned intuitively
Subtitle 'Custom dishes appear in food search when you log meals' explains the feature purpose well
bad: Despite being named '08-dishes-empty', the screenshot actually shows one dish — the empty state with just the subtitle text is underselling the feature's discoverability
The single dish card has no visual state for 'last used' or 'times logged' — lacks engagement hooks
No search or filter functionality visible for when dishes list grows — scalability concern
Card macro row (P/C/F colored dots) uses very small text that may be unreadable on mobile at actual device resolution
The page has excessive whitespace below the single card with no suggested next actions
improvements: Add a true empty state illustration (chef hat or bowl icon) with headline 'Create your first dish' and body text explaining the benefit for the zero-dish state
Add 'Last used: 2 days ago' or 'Logged 5 times' metadata to dish cards to increase engagement and utility
Increase macro label text to text-sm and add full labels on hover/tap (e.g., 'Protein: 13g' instead of just '13.0g P')
Add a subtle 'Add another dish' ghost button card at the end of the list to encourage content creation
Consider a 2-column grid layout on wider screens for better use of horizontal space

pages.name: 09-dishes-create
good: Green focus ring on the dish name input clearly signals active state
Two-phase UX (name first, then search ingredients) is logically structured
Cancel/Save button row with clear color differentiation (gray vs green) follows standard modal conventions
Placeholder examples in both fields reduce cognitive load
bad: The ingredient search area has a large empty void with just an icon and text — no visual indication of what an added ingredient will look like
No diet type selector (Veg/Non-Veg/Vegan) on the create form — but the list view shows a 'veg' badge, creating a data input gap
Save Dish button is active even when no ingredients are added — should be disabled with a tooltip explaining why
DISH NAME and INGREDIENTS labels are in all-caps small tracking text which reduces readability at small sizes
No serving size or total weight input visible — unclear how the per-100g calculation will be defined at creation time
Modal lacks a close (X) button in the top right corner — only Cancel at bottom, which requires scrolling on mobile
improvements: Add a Veg/Non-Veg/Vegan toggle (pill selector) below the dish name field to capture diet type at creation
Disable the Save Dish button (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) until at least one ingredient is added, with a tooltip 'Add at least one ingredient'
Add a preview of ingredient row (e.g., a ghost skeleton card) in the empty ingredient area to show users what to expect
Add a close X button (top-right, text-gray-400 hover:text-white) to the modal header for quick dismissal
Add a 'Total serving size' field with default 100g and allow custom input so users can define a full dish portion

top_fixes: Add proper empty states with illustrations and CTA buttons across all zero-data screens (tracker, dishes) — current text-only empty states make the app feel unfinished and reduce feature discoverability

Establish a consistent typography scale: consumed/primary values should be text-2xl to text-4xl font-bold text-white, while labels and denominators should be text-xs to text-sm text-gray-400 — currently all text weights feel too similar
Make macro progress bars visible at zero state by adding a full-width track (bg-gray-700 rounded-full h-2) so the goal container is always visible, preventing the current 'broken' appearance

Add Indian meal structure (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks sections) to the food tracker — this is the highest-impact localization improvement for the target Indian user base and reduces cognitive load in logging

Add a Veg/Non-Veg/Vegan diet badge toggle to the dish creation form and ensure it propagates correctly to the dish card — the current gap between creation form and display card creates user trust issues around data accuracy

pages.name: 01-dashboard
good: Calorie ring with centered KCAL value creates strong focal point and clear hierarchy
Green brand color used purposefully for remaining calories and streak elements
Water intake section with circular progress and quick-add buttons is highly functional
Weight progress chart with goal line dashed in green is contextually informative
Card-based layout with consistent bg-gray-900 creates clean sectioning
bad: BMI (19.8) and day streak (0) are displayed at same visual weight as primary calorie data — no hierarchy differentiation
Right panel with BMI and streak feels cramped and under-spaced compared to the dominant calorie card
Water intake quick-add buttons (150ml, 250ml, 500ml, 750ml) have identical styling with no visual differentiation for most common action
'Next milestone' card uses very similar dark green background that barely separates it from other cards — low contrast
Macros section shows all zeros with no skeleton/placeholder state — feels abandoned
Weight chart y-axis labels (57-61) are extremely small and low contrast, barely legible
improvements: Increase BMI value to text-2xl and add a colored pill badge (green for healthy) directly on the stat instead of plain text below
Add a subtle divider or increase padding between calorie ring card and BMI/streak card to reduce cramped feel — target min 16px gap
Highlight the 250ml Glass button with a slightly brighter border or scale-up to indicate most common serving
Increase weight chart label font size to at least 11px and use text-gray-400 for better legibility
Add a faint progress indicator or 'Log your first meal' micro-CTA inside the macros zeros to reduce empty feeling

pages.name: 02-tracker-today
good: Quick Add section with Indian meal presets (Dal Tadka, Roti, Idli) is an excellent localization touch and solves the blank-state problem proactively
Date navigator with back/forward arrows and TODAY badge is clean and highly usable
Macro breakdown (Protein/Carbs/Fat) with both current and goal values in the bottom summary bar is information-dense but readable
Empty state illustration with salad bowl and + icon plus CTA button is polished and encouraging
Search bar with contextual placeholder text (e.g. Dal Tadka, Roti, Rice...) reinforces Indian food context
bad: Quick Add cards have inconsistent emoji sizes relative to card size — some emojis appear small and misaligned vertically
Calorie count under Quick Add items (180 kcal, 70 kcal) uses same text-gray-400 as labels — calorie number should be more prominent
The nutrition totals bar at bottom shows 0g / 150g but the progress bars are all flat gray — no visual differentiation between protein/carbs/fat bar colors
Settings gear icon in top right has no tooltip or label — unclear what it controls
2000 kcal goal in the tracker differs from 2454 kcal shown in the dashboard — inconsistency creates confusion
improvements: Color-code the three macro progress bars: blue for protein, yellow/amber for carbs, orange for fat — matching dashboard macro colors
Make kcal values in Quick Add cards text-white font-semibold instead of gray to create secondary hierarchy within the card
Align calorie display: ensure food log page uses the same 2454 kcal goal as the dashboard, not 2000 kcal
Add a tooltip or label on the gear icon: 'Log Settings' — or replace with a more contextual icon like a filter
Add a meal-type segmentation (Breakfast / Lunch / Dinner / Snacks) above the empty state to give users structure before they log

pages.name: 05-profile
good: Stats badge row (BMI, Maintenance, Your Goal, Protein, Carbs, Fat) provides an at-a-glance summary — the green highlight on Your Goal badge is effective
Identity card with avatar initial, age, gender, height is clean and well-structured
BMI 19.8 — Healthy weight green inline label adds positive reinforcement
Form layout for Update Goals with clear field labels is functional and unambiguous
Dev Mode Active banner is clearly differentiated with green/red text — good developer UX
bad: Avatar is just a letter 'J' in a circle — no option to add photo creates a generic feel that reduces personal connection
Stats badges have no units on some values (184g Protein reads oddly — should clarify it is a daily target)
The goals form (Current Weight, Goal Weight, Timeline, Activity Level, Diet Type) is vertically long with no visual grouping or section labels between fields
Sign Out button uses red outline style but is positioned below Account section with insufficient separation from Re-do Onboarding — they could be accidentally confused
Dropdowns (Timeline, Activity Level, Diet Type) use default browser styling — inconsistent with the custom dark theme
improvements: Add a camera icon overlay on the avatar circle to hint at photo upload capability, even if future feature — increases perceived completeness
Group goals form fields into two visual sections: 'Weight Goals' (current/goal weight) and 'Lifestyle' (activity, diet, timeline) with small section headers in text-xs text-gray-500 uppercase
Replace native select dropdowns with custom-styled dark dropdowns matching bg-gray-800 border-gray-700 to maintain design consistency
Add subtitle text under each stat badge: e.g. 'Daily Target' under Protein/Carbs/Fat to clarify context
Increase vertical spacing between the Re-do Onboarding list item and Sign Out button to at least 24px, and consider moving Sign Out to a destructive zone at the very bottom with a separator

pages.name: 08-dishes-empty
good: Logged 5 times counter on the dish card provides useful engagement data
Veg/non-veg tag badge in green is a culturally relevant and visually clear affordance for Indian users
Macro breakdown (P/C/F) inline on the dish card is compact and informative
Add another dish dashed-border button is a clean affordance that implies the list pattern
bad: The page title says 'empty state (no dishes created yet)' but the screenshot actually shows one dish (meal 1) — the empty state design itself is not visible, making evaluation harder; the 'empty' concept is not communicated with an illustration or zero-state message
Edit and delete icons on the dish card are very small (approximately 16px) and closely spaced — touch targets too small for mobile, high misclick risk
The helper text 'Custom dishes appear in food search when you log meals' is low contrast and positioned below the fold — most users will miss this key piece of information
Dish card shows '202 kcal /100g' but the base quantity notation is unclear — users may not understand if this is per serving or per 100g
No search or filter functionality visible — becomes a usability problem as dish count grows
improvements: Increase edit/delete icon touch targets to minimum 44x44px by wrapping in a larger clickable area with p-3 padding
Move the 'Custom dishes appear in food search' helper text to a highlighted info card at the top of the page with an info icon — treat it as onboarding context
Clarify nutrition display: change '202 kcal /100g' to '202 kcal per serving (300g)' to remove ambiguity about the base unit
Add a search bar at the top of My Dishes page above the dish list to support future scalability
For true empty state: add a chef illustration or icon with heading 'No dishes yet' and subtext 'Create your first custom recipe' above the New Dish CTA

pages.name: 09-dishes-create
good: Green border focus state on the dish name input is clear and on-brand
Two-step mental model (name first, then search ingredients) is logical and reduces cognitive load
Chef hat icon placeholder in ingredients area is a nice empty-state micro-detail
Cancel/Save Dish button pair with contrasting styles (gray vs green) clearly communicates the primary action
Placeholder text examples (e.g. My Poha, Mom's Dal, Evening Smoothie) are culturally resonant and helpful
bad: Form is a floating card but has no visible modal overlay/backdrop — feels disconnected from the page context without a dimmed background
Ingredients section label 'INGREDIENTS — search and add' uses a long inline label that would be better as a label + subtitle separated
No diet type selector (Veg/Non-Veg) in the create form — but the dish list shows a veg badge, meaning this data is required but not collected here
Save Dish button is active even with empty fields — should be disabled (opacity-50 cursor-not-allowed) until both name and at least one ingredient are filled
No character counter on dish name field — users don't know length limits, risk creating truncated names
improvements: Add a Veg / Non-Veg toggle (pill toggle style, green for veg, orange for non-veg) below the dish name input to collect diet type during creation
Disable the Save Dish button until dish name is non-empty and at least one ingredient is added — use opacity-40 and cursor-not-allowed states
Add a semi-transparent dark backdrop (bg-black/60) behind the create card if it is a modal, or restructure as a full page with a back button for clarity
Split ingredient label into: label text 'INGREDIENTS' on its own line, then a softer subtitle 'Search and add foods below' in text-gray-400 text-sm
Add a serving size / total portions input field below the ingredients list so nutrition can be calculated per serving rather than just total

top_fixes: Unify calorie goal across all pages — the Food Log shows 2000 kcal while Dashboard shows 2454 kcal; this inconsistency destroys user trust and must be a single source of truth from the profile goal calculation

Color-code all macro bars consistently across Dashboard and Food Tracker (blue=protein, amber=carbs, orange=fat) — currently macros are indistinguishable gray bars that convey no information at a glance

Replace all native browser select dropdowns (Profile goals form, Diet Type) with custom dark-themed dropdowns matching bg-gray-800 border border-gray-700 rounded-lg to close the most obvious design inconsistency in the app

Add a Veg/Non-Veg toggle to the dish creation form and increase edit/delete touch targets on dish cards to 44px minimum — both are critical UX gaps that affect daily usability for Indian users who filter by diet type

Disable Save/submit buttons until required fields are filled across all forms (dish creation, food log) and add micro-validation feedback — currently all CTAs are always active which leads to empty submissions and poor error states

pages.name: 01-dashboard
good: Calorie ring with centered KCAL label creates clear focal hierarchy
BMI badge with 'Healthy' green pill is immediately scannable and reassuring
Water intake section with 37% circular progress and quick-add buttons is highly functional
Weight chart with goal dashed line gives clear visual context
Two-column layout for ring + streak/BMI card uses space efficiently
bad: Macro bars in 'Today's Macros' are all at 0% and look identical — no visual differentiation between Protein/Carbs/Fat bars, making the section feel inert and broken
The calorie ring at 0/2454 shows a completely empty gray ring with no baseline arc, which looks unfinished rather than 'ready to fill'
Water quick-add buttons (+150ml, +250ml etc.) are too uniformly styled — no visual weight difference between them, making the section feel flat
'Next milestone' banner text is small and the 18-weeks-away sub-label is easy to miss — low contrast secondary text
The dashboard has no greeting personalization beyond the name — no motivational nudge, streak context, or day-of-week meal suggestion
improvements: Add a subtle baseline arc (5% filled, gray-700) to the calorie ring when at 0 so it reads as 'empty vessel' not 'broken component'
Color-code macro progress bars: blue for Protein, amber for Carbs, orange for Fat — matching the label colors already used in the tracker page
Increase water quick-add button contrast: make the active/primary size (250ml) slightly larger or highlighted in green to guide default action
Increase milestone sub-text to text-sm text-gray-300 and add a small progress bar showing 0% → 50% → goal to make it feel alive
Add a subtle card separator or section label between the ring card and macro card to break the visual monotony of stacked dark cards

pages.name: 02-tracker-today
good: Date navigator with TODAY badge is clean and immediately scannable
Quick Add grid of popular Indian meals (Dal Tadka, Roti, Idli etc.) is a killer localization feature — highly relevant
Three-column macro display (Protein/Carbs/Fat) with colored labels is clear and color-consistent
Empty state illustration with 'Log your first meal' CTA is charming and contextually appropriate
Search bar placeholder 'e.g. Dal Tadka, Roti, Rice...' sets perfect Indian-context expectations
bad: Quick Add meal cards have no calorie-to-visual ratio — all 6 cards look identical in weight regardless of calorie count, missing an opportunity for scannable density cues
The macro targets (150g protein, 250g carbs, 65g fat) shown as small gray text are too subtle — users won't notice their daily targets at a glance
No meal-time segmentation (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks) — all foods will log into one undifferentiated list, which is a significant UX gap for meal planning
The calorie progress bar under '0 / 2454 kcal' is nearly invisible at 0% — just a thin gray line that adds no value
Quick Add section header 'Popular Indian meals' is right-aligned in muted gray — easy to miss as a section label
improvements: Add meal-time sections (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, Snacks) as collapsible groups with add buttons per section — this is standard in MyFitnessPal and dramatically improves usability
Make macro target numbers more prominent: show '0g / 150g' in a pill or inline badge rather than just small gray text below the bar
Add a subtle calorie-density indicator to Quick Add cards (e.g. a thin colored left border: green for <150kcal, amber for 150-250kcal, red for >250kcal)
Increase the progress bar height to h-2 and add a green fill even at 1% logged so it feels responsive immediately after first log
Move 'Popular Indian meals' label to left-align with uppercase tracking-wider styling to match section header conventions

pages.name: 05-profile
good: Stats badges row (BMI, TDEE, Goal kcal, Protein, Carbs, Fat) gives a dense but scannable stats overview
Goal kcal badge highlighted with green border creates clear visual emphasis on the user's active target
Form layout for weight goals and lifestyle is clean with consistent dark input styling
BMI 19.8 Healthy weight in cyan text below badges is a nice reassurance micro-detail
Re-do Onboarding option in Account section is a useful escape hatch for users who entered wrong data
bad: The profile avatar is just a letter 'J' in a green circle — no option to upload a photo or choose an avatar style, which feels incomplete for a profile page
Stats badges use three different text colors (white, blue/cyan, amber, orange) with no legend explaining what the colors mean — creates confusion
Dev Mode Active banner at the bottom is bright red/orange and visually dominates the page — should be hidden or extremely subtle in screenshots/demos
The form section header 'UPDATE GOALS' is in small uppercase gray text with no visual separation from the stats section above — poor section delineation
Sign Out button has a red border but sits inside the ACCOUNT card without enough visual weight — it should be more clearly destructive/separated
improvements: Add a thin horizontal divider (border-gray-800) and 16px extra padding between the stats card and the UPDATE GOALS form card to improve section separation
Add a small legend or tooltip on stats badge colors: e.g. hover/tap shows 'Protein daily target' — or simply add the unit label in consistent gray below each colored value
Move Sign Out button outside the ACCOUNT card as a standalone full-width red-tinted button at the bottom with mt-8 spacing to signal it's a destructive action
Add initials avatar with a tap-to-edit overlay (camera icon) even if photo upload isn't supported yet — improves perceived completeness
Suppress or replace Dev Mode banner with a tiny dev indicator (dot in corner) that doesn't visually break the page layout

pages.name: 08-dishes-empty
good: Info banner explaining 'Custom dishes appear in food search' is excellent onboarding copy — solves the 'why should I do this' question proactively
Green info icon on the banner creates visual association with the brand without being aggressive
The dish card showing 'meal 1' with macro breakdown (13g P, 11.8g C, 11.1g F) and 'Logged 5 times' badge demonstrates the feature value clearly
Edit and delete icons on the dish card are appropriately subtle — icon-only with ample hit target
Search bar for dishes shows filtering intent is supported even with one item
bad: The filename says 'dishes-empty' but the screenshot actually shows one dish — this creates confusion; the true empty state with zero dishes is not shown
The 'Add another dish' dashed border button at bottom is extremely low contrast — the dashed border on dark background is almost invisible
Macro labels (P, C, F) are single-letter abbreviations without a legend — new users won't know what they mean without prior context
No sorting or filtering controls beyond search — as the list grows, there's no way to sort by recent/most logged/calories
The dish card doesn't show a veg/non-veg color dot in the title area — the 'veg' badge is green text but small and easy to overlook
improvements: Increase 'Add another dish' button visibility: use border-2 border-dashed border-gray-600 with text-gray-400 and a subtle hover:border-green-500 transition
Expand P/C/F abbreviations to 'Protein · Carbs · Fat' on first render or add a one-time tooltip to educate new users
Add a veg/non-veg colored left border to dish cards (green for veg, red for non-veg) as a common Indian food app convention that aids instant recognition
Add sort dropdown (Most logged / Recently added / Calories: low-high) in the top-right area next to the search bar
Make the 'Logged 5 times' badge more prominent with a subtle background pill (bg-gray-700) so it reads as a stat rather than plain text

pages.name: 09-dishes-create
good: Two-step form structure (name → ingredients) is logical and minimal — no cognitive overload
Green focus ring on the DISH NAME input clearly shows active state and is on-brand
Ingredient search placeholder matches the food log search placeholder — consistent UX pattern
Cancel/Save Dish button pair with clear visual hierarchy (gray vs green) follows standard form conventions
Empty ingredient state message 'Search above to add your first ingredient' is helpful inline guidance
bad: The form has no serving size field — users can't define what '1 serving' means for their dish, making the per-serving nutrition calculation opaque
No diet type toggle (Veg/Non-Veg) on the create form — it should be set here, not inferred, especially since the dish list shows veg badges
The ingredient search returns no visual preview of what will be added — there's no quantity input shown, so users don't know they'll need to specify grams
The modal/card has very generous padding but no visible max-width constraint behavior — on wider screens this could stretch awkwardly
No character counter on the DISH NAME field and no indication of max length or naming conventions
improvements: Add a 'Diet Type' toggle (Veg / Non-Veg / Egg) as a two-button segmented control below the dish name field — this is essential for Indian food context
Add a 'Total Serving Size (g)' input field with a helper text 'e.g. 300g for one bowl' so users understand how per-serving nutrition is computed
Show a real-time nutrition summary card at the bottom of the ingredients list that updates as ingredients are added — this is the #1 motivator to add more ingredients accurately
Add a subtle character count (e.g. 0/50) below the dish name input and show validation error inline if left empty on save attempt
Add a micro-animation or slide-in for the ingredient row when an item is added from search — improves perceived responsiveness of the form

top_fixes: Add meal-time segmentation (Breakfast/Lunch/Dinner/Snacks) to the food tracker — this is the single biggest functional UX gap and is expected by users of any serious calorie tracker
Color-code macro progress bars consistently across dashboard and tracker (blue/Protein, amber/Carbs, orange/Fat) — currently the dashboard bars are uniform gray making them meaningless at a glance
Add Veg/Non-Veg/Egg diet type indicator to the dish create form and use colored left-border convention on dish cards — a critical Indian food app localization that builds trust with target users

Fix the 'Add another dish' dashed button and all near-invisible UI elements on dark backgrounds — increase border opacity to gray-600 minimum and ensure all interactive elements meet 3:1 contrast ratio against bg-gray-900
Add a real-time nutrition summary panel in the dish create form that updates as ingredients are added — transforms the create flow from data-entry task into an engaging nutrition-building experience

fixes.summary: Round 1: improved 11 files targeting top issues
Round 2: improved 11 files targeting top issues
